footer.dataSources: Paper metadata from <oa>OpenAlex</oa> (CC0) and <cr>Crossref</cr> (CC0); source entries from <ax>arXiv</ax>.

notFound.title: Page not found
notFound.copy: This route is not part of the web workspace.

home.title: QuantumAtlas
home.subtitle: Browse extracted papers, inspect primitives, and hand API-ready tokens to trusted terminals.

wiki.title: Structured knowledge base
wiki.subtitle: Pages are read from the Wiki Git checkout and rendered by the web app.
wiki.searchSubtitle: Search titles, tags, page ids, and extracted content.

papers.title: RAG semantic search
papers.subtitle: Ask in natural language; retrieves the most relevant chunks across ~89k papers / 3.2M chunks via hybrid (dense + sparse) search and reranks them.
papers.inputPlaceholder: e.g. What is the complexity bound for quantum phase estimation?
papers.rerankHint: Cross-encoder rerank (slower but better top-k quality)
papers.useSparseHint: Add BM25 sparse recall on top of dense retrieval (hybrid)
queryExamples: How does the surface code distance d affect logical error rate
Complexity bound for quantum phase estimation
Convergence bottlenecks of VQE on chemistry systems
papers.resultsHeader: {{count}} results · took {{took}}s{{reranked, select, true{ · reranked} other{}}}
papers.noResults: No matching chunks. Try rephrasing or relaxing filters.
papers.unavailableBody: The server has no RAG sidecar configured, or the sidecar is unhealthy. For local dev see VITE_DEV_RAG_SIDECAR in web/.env.development.example; for production see QATLAS_RAG_SIDECAR_URL in .env.example.
papers.degradedBody: The vector store or reranker is partially unavailable; results may be inaccurate.

graph.title: Knowledge graph overview
graph.subtitle: Neo4j-backed topology and schema health for the quantum knowledge graph.
graph.nodeDetailCopy: Graph node detail pages now live in the web shell. Add a dedicated JSON endpoint when richer node payloads are needed.

token._removed: Session-token page was removed; the PAT page covers all CLI bearer-token use cases.

pat.title: Personal Access Tokens
pat.subtitle: Long-lived bearer tokens for CLI, CI, and other automation. Each PAT carries an explicit scope set (default deny) and a mandatory expiry (max 365 days), modelled after GitHub fine-grained PATs.
pat.noScopesWarning: With no scopes selected, this token will be rejected (403) by every write endpoint.
pat.noScopesBadge: no scopes (can't call any write endpoint)

fields.name: Name (required)
fields.scopes: Scopes (defaults to all — uncheck what this token should not be allowed to do)

pat.revokeConfirm: Revoke token "{{name}}"? This cannot be undone — any script using it will start getting 401 immediately.

issued.title: Copy your token now
issued.warning: This is the only time you will see this value. After closing this dialog the server only keeps a one-way hash — there is no way to recover the plaintext.

device.title: Authorize a device
device.subtitle: A qatlas CLI on another device asked you to authorize it. Enter the short code it printed, review the token it's requesting, then approve or deny.
device.consent: Approving will mint a Personal Access Token under your account and hand it back to the requesting CLI. Only do this if you started the request yourself.
device.approvedBody: The CLI's next poll will receive the token. You can safely close this tab.
device.deniedBody: The CLI will exit with an access_denied error. No token was issued.

approved.title: Already approved
approved.body: This code was already approved — the CLI should have picked up the token.

consumed.title: Already used
consumed.body: This code was already approved and the CLI has already collected its token.

denied.title: Already denied
denied.body: This code was already denied; no token will be issued for it.

expired.title: Expired
expired.body: This code has expired. Re-run `qatlas auth login --device` to start over.

login.title: QuantumAtlas
login.subtitle: Sign in with your GitHub account to continue.
login.footer: You will be redirected to github.com to authorize, then sent back here automatically.

auth.exchanging: Exchanging the GitHub code with PocketBase.
auth.failedReturn: <loginLink>Return to the sign-in page</loginLink> and try again.
auth.missingCode: Missing code or state in OAuth callback URL.
